meaning: n.索邦神学院(巴黎大学的前身),巴黎大学
examples: Irene entered Sorbonne University in October 1914 to prepare for her degree in mathematics and physics.
When World War I began, she left Sorbonne University to help her mother, who was using X-ray facilities to help save the lives of wounded soldiers.
On May 13, 1906, she was appointed to the professorship that had been left vacant on her husband's death, becoming the first woman to teach at the Sorbonne.
Edward studied Literature at Oxford University and Philosophy and Economics at the Sorbonne, Paris.
For help he turns to a young woman, a Sorbonne-trained historian who is also a griotte.
